title: Major Media Reviews of Tampa Opener Published — Rolling Stone Notes 'Magic Shop' Return for First Time Since 2019
detail: On April 27, 2026 — a scheduled rest day between Tampa Night 2 (April 26) and Night 3 (April 28) — major outlets publish in-depth coverage of the ARIRANG World Tour North America opener. Rolling Stone's review of the April 25 opener highlights the emotional return of 'Magic Shop,' a fan-beloved track performed for the first time since 2019, describing the moment as 'among the most affecting in recent BTS concert history.' The Tampa Bay Times publishes a '4 things we loved about BTS in Tampa' feature covering: the Beatlemania-scale fan atmosphere with attendees flying in from across North America; the 360-degree production scale and setlist arc bridging ARIRANG material with classic BTS; RM's seated-but-spirited performance following his ankle injury; and 'Permission to Dance' marking its first live performance since the Las Vegas run in 2022. Both reviews cite the Tampa run as BTS's definitive stadium-scale reset — not merely a reunion tour but an artistic statement. Meanwhile, the Cafe Arirang Tour fan pop-up event (presented by Honeii_co and KXP) concludes its final day at GANGCHU, 6618 North Nebraska Avenue in Tampa, with thousands of ARMY gathering for commemorative merchandise and Korean food ahead of Night 3.

media.caption: Rolling Stone reviews BTS's Tampa opener, noting the emotional return of 'Magic Shop' for the first time since 2019

title: Three More ARIRANG Tracks Chart on Billboard Hot 100 — 'Hooligan,' '2.0,' 'Body to Body' All Bubbling
detail: As the ARIRANG World Tour's North America leg rolls through Tampa, Billboard chart data released around April 27 shows three additional ARIRANG album tracks charting on the Billboard Hot 100 alongside lead single 'SWIM' (No. 10): 'Hooligan' (No. 90), '2.0' (No. 88), and 'Body to Body' (No. 69). The concurrent charting of four tracks from ARIRANG on the Hot 100 underscores the album's sustained commercial momentum in its fifth week — rare for any act and unprecedented for a K-pop group in album cycle week 5. 'SWIM' also holds No. 1 on Digital Song Sales for a fourth consecutive week and No. 13 on Pop Radio Airplay (its new peak, the highest ever for a Korean act). The simultaneous charting mirrors the album's Week 1 feat when all 14 tracks occupied the top 14 spots on Spotify's Global Top 50.

media.caption: Multiple ARIRANG tracks chart simultaneously on the Billboard Hot 100 in week 5 of the album's cycle
